title: Nuclei Isolation from Human Skeletal Muscle
author: Hongbo Zhang
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.t68erhw

steps.instruction: Prepare 5ml Buffer A and 10ml Buffer B for each human sample
Dissect all the hind limb muscles and place in a 35/60mm tissue culture dish with 0.5ml PBS.
Remove the fat from muscle using a scissors, and wash the muscle with 1X PBS twice.
Mechanically chop the muscle into 1mm pieces with a razor, mix the red and white muscles.
Rinse the 7 ml dounce homogenizer with MiliQ water.
Transfer the 0.3g of the mashed muscle into a 7 ml dounce homogenizer with 3ml of Buffer A using the razor (the rest of the muscle will be used for single cell isolation).

Ground the tissue on ice for 50 strokes with the loose Pestle A (clearance of 0.114 mm), try to avoid bubbles. Do not twist the pestle while pushing the pestle down. It might cause the nuclei degradation.

Filter the mixture passed through a 100micron strainer on top of a 50ml falcon tube. Rinse the dounce homogenizer with 1ml Buffer A and then wash through the cell strainer, collect into the 50ml falcon tube.
Repeat the washing with another 750ul of Buffer A. The combined tissue homogenate in the 50ml falcon tube now should be ±4.75ml.
add 250ul of Triton X-100 (10%) to the tissue homogenate to make the final concentration of Triton X-100 is 0.5%. Mix the solution gently by pipetting.
Wash the 7 ml dounce homogenizer with MiliQ water for the next step.
Placed the tissue homogenate back into a cleaned dounce homogenizer.
Ground the mixture an additional 50 strokes with the tight Pestle B (clearance of 0.076mm).
Filter the mixture through a 40micron strainer. Leave the bubbles in the strainer and collect the filtered solution that accumulated on the bottom of the strainer.
Centrifuge the tissue filtrate at 3000 x g for 5min at 4 degrees to get nuclei pellet. Remove the supernatant entirely.
Re-suspend the nuclei pellet in 1ml Buffer B and transfer to a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube.
Prepare 4 new Eppendorf tubes, add 475 ul of Buffer B into each tube.
Gently mix the nuclei suspension by pipetting, and then take 225 ul of nuclei suspension and 300 ul of Percoll solution into each tube to create a 27% Percoll-nuclei solution. Mix by pipetting.
Centrifuge the Eppendorf tube at 20,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4 degrees, remove the supernatant and keep only the nuclei layer (pellet), note the tube orientation.
Re-suspend the nuclear layer into 200ul of Buffer B. Pool the nuclei suspensions from 4 tubes to one tube.
Spin down the nuclei at 2500 x g for 3 minutes at 4C.
Remove the supernatant and add 1ml of buffer B. Gently disperse the pellet with pipetting and spin down the nuclei at 2500 x g for 3 minutes at 4C.
Remove the supernatant and re-suspend the nuclei in 50-200 ul of Buffer B (the volume need to be adjusted according to the size of the pellet).
Stain the intact nuclei using trypan blue (50:50) and quantify by counting with a hemocytometer.
Wash the pestles and homogenizers with water-80% EtOH and leave to dry out till next experiment.
